behavior_summary: U8P_MAGLEGS::look is the item's U8P_MAGLEGS::look/inspect handler. When the player examines this item the script emits a single player-facing bark reading 'magic leggings ' and then the process ends (suspend/return).
There is no conditional logic, menu interaction, or external calls in this U8P_MAGLEGS::look routine; it solely presents the short descriptive label for the item.
The item file contains gameplay properties (armourClass = 4, kickBonus = 3) but those are not referenced by U8P_MAGLEGS::look.
notable_control_flow: Single linear flow: enter U8P_MAGLEGS::look process -> emit Bark('magic leggings ') -> suspend/terminate process.
No branches, flags, or calls alter execution in this routine.
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the bark text 'magic leggings ' intentional or an artifact of extraction?
Are the item gameplay fields (armourClass, kickBonus) used by other processes not present in this excerpt (e.g., equip/use handlers)?
Does this item have other processes (equip, use, destroy) in separate scripts that rely on global flags or external calls?

main_function.name: U8P_MAGLEGS::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description shown to the player when they examine the item.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the player 'looks at' or inspects the item (standard U8P_MAGLEGS::look/inspect process).

function_map.name: U8P_MAGLEGS::look
function_map.role: Emit short descriptive bark when the player examines the item
